name: step-indicator
title: Step indicator
description: Multi-step progress indicator: a <nav> landmark wrapping an ordered list of steps. Status (complete / current / incomplete) is derived from currentStep. The current step carries aria-current=step; every step also carries a visually hidden status word and a non-color marker (checkmark or ring), so progress never depends on color alone. Optional 'Step X of Y' counter; on narrow screens only the current label stays visible while assistive tech still reads them all.
useWhen: Showing progress through a fixed, ordered, multi-step flow (application, wizard, checkout)
Orienting the user to which step they are on and how many remain
A linear process where steps are known ahead of time
avoidWhen: A single-step form (no progress to show)
Free navigation between unordered sections (use tabs or in-page navigation)
An indeterminate or open-ended process (use a status message or spinner)
As the primary navigation control — this communicates status, it is not a menu

keyboard: The indicator communicates status and is not interactive, so it adds no keyboard behavior of its own
It is exposed as a navigation landmark (<nav> with an accessible name) that screen-reader users can jump to
The current step is programmatically marked with aria-current=step
Each step announces a spelled-out status word (completed / current / not completed) so progress is never color-only
